title: Adding single users/ seat
excerpt: We have 30 users currently and just added a new member to the team. This felt like great news but we cannot justify adding 10 extra seats for an entire year when we will only be using one. It’s outrageous and completely …

posts.author: Jack (Jack Stirk)
posts.cookedHtml:
We have 30 users currently and just added a new member to the team. This felt like great news but we cannot justify adding 10 extra seats for an entire year when we will only be using one. It’s outrageous and completely unnecessary to pay almost an extra £2k for one user. Most software allows you to add single users freely, paying for the amount you use feels logical and fair. We simply cannot sign off budget to add extra licences if we’re not going to be using most of them so it’s a real sticking point on whether we are able continue, which seems such a shame.

It’s also silly that when you’re on annual, to add more seats you have to pay for another year which resets your annual billing date. It would be much better to pay a pro-rata fee for the extra seats from now, until your annual renewal date. Then when you’re billing date arrives you pay for those extra users for an entire year. This is how it works with almost all other software I’ve used over the years. We can’t just pay up for a full year again and again when we want to add more users as we have budgets to work within.

We’re happy with the software and it seems such a shame that these pricing issues are getting in the way of us growing our team in the software. Please look into this Monday as we’d have to have to leave because we’re being forced to pay for user licences we won’t use.

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Jack, thank you so much for your honesty and feedback.

We really do appreciate hearing your thoughts on our pricing model and experience with our platform. We are passing along your perspective to our team but in the meantime, I was wondering if your team had tried utilizing free Guest seats on your plan.

I completely understand this is not ideal, but many organizations find that a smaller plan size and using the free Guest seats allows them to cut costs on the monday subscription.

This doesn’t solve the problem but we’d love to know if this might be helpful in the meantime. Please do let us know!

posts.author: pk.afsda (Kurt Osena)
posts.cookedHtml:
As someone who uses this as an individual, it would be good if there was a 1-2 seat upgrade that unlocks advanced features without having to upgrade to a 3+ seat account. I love using this app for personal organization, scheduling, and creating budgets etc. However, as an individual you are sort of forced to have to upgrade to a team account, even if you do not have one, to be able to use advance features. It would be nice to have accommodations for those who do that not have a team, by creating an individual paid account to unlock the same advance features as teams that pay are able to access, but at a price that is reasonable for 1 person, possibly 2 (for a partner/spouse).

I am happy to pay monthly, if there was an upgrade for the 1-2 seat individual account at a price point that makes sense for individual use. Thank you.

As someone who uses this as an individual, it would be good if there was a 1-2 seat upgrade that unlocks advanced features without having to upgrade to a 3+ seat account. I love using this app for personal organization, scheduling, and creating budgets etc. However, as an individual you are sort of forced to have to upgrade to a team account, even if you do not have one, to be able to use advance features. It would be nice to have accommodations for those who do that not have a team, by creating an individual paid account to unlock the same advance features as teams that pay are able to access, but at a price that is reasonable for 1 person, possibly 2 (for a partner/spouse).

I am happy to pay monthly, if there was an upgrade for the 1-2 seat individual account at a price point that makes sense for individual use. Thank you.

posts.author: Bastouri (Daniel Bastouri)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agreed.

I know Monday.com will say that it is intended as a team app, and it absolutely works wonders as a team app, but it is also such a strong app for individuals and I would love to see an individual option at the enterprise package level.

I ended up having to buy 3 accounts even though I am the only person using it. I bought it for CRM purposes, but it was so expensive to go with 3 accounts when all I use is one.

Hopefully it gets released as an individual plan as Monday continues to grow!

posts.author: pk.afsda (Kurt Osena)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, I hope so too! Thank you for sharing, it is good to know I’m not the only one feeling this way.

posts.author: Bastouri (Daniel Bastouri)
posts.cookedHtml:
Absolutely haha. I think they are just very focused on the team aspect for now. Oh well!

Cheers,

Daniel

posts.author: pk.afsda (Kurt Osena)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, I have found that to be the case as well. I sent a message to a representative and their only option for me at the time was to look for people to fill those seats.

Here are reasons I think Monday.com should consider our suggestion:

  1. I think this would only help them out as I am sure there are others like ourselves that love the app, but are individual users.

  2. It doesn’t make sense to have an individual account option, but then limit the individual from using the app to its potential unless they pay for three seats (even though they may not have 2 people to fill the others seats). I think if they decide to keep it as is, then they should rename the individual account to not say “individual”. I think it’s a bit disappointing for an individual to sign up to use Monday.com and then have to work more to keep using it (i.e. invite others to use it to unlock more items).

  3. Their competition (i.e. Clickup) have plans that accommodate individual users, while they provide group plans like Monday.com. Therefore, this would prevent individual users from needing to switch to the competition to get their needs satisfied.

Btw, I share this as a fan of Monday.com and someone who believes in it’s appeal to individuals, if only they considered making the suggested adjustment.

posts.author: mchinsky (Mark Chinsky)
posts.cookedHtml: Yea there are a number of pricing/bundling decisions that make no sense. For example, since you must use automations for recurring entries and handling completed tasks (which is native to say ClickUp), the 250 automations/month on the standard plan is useless so virtually everyone needs $16/month/user (annual) Pro

posts.author: pk.afsda (Kurt Osena)
posts.cookedHtml: Hm, that’s a good point. I didn’t think about that. Thanks for bringing that up as well. It might be good for them to evaluate their price plans in consideration of these frustrations.

posts.author: toomanybarts (Bart S)
posts.cookedHtml:
In exact same spot. Used it at my last job fir large collaborations.
Have now set up on my own and using as CRM. Don’t want to pay for 3 seats for just me.
Now looking at Asana instead.

The reasons given are obtuse. I’d feel better if they just said “economically it doesn’t make sense for us for single users as you have the most support questions etc etc” or something.

posts.author: zacheryy (Zachery Young)
posts.cookedHtml: EXACTLY!! I spoke to someone that manages my company’s account and my only option was to join the marketing teams account - I do work with that team so it doesn’t make any sense for me. It’s also silly because I would spend the same/more to gain additional features if 1 seat accounts were an option - but not since i have to pay for 3 that go unused.

posts.author: pk.afsda (Kurt Osena)
posts.cookedHtml:
Unfortunately, I am coming close to maximizing my allotted items before I would need to invite others so that I can continue using individual. However, I don’t see how working more to continue using the individual account makes any sense, especially when the whole purpose of using Monday.com is suppose to make work more efficient. Also, I don’t know that I would want to invite anyone to use this for individual purposes, if they will eventually get stuck in the same dilemma. So I’m having to look at other options as well.

I’m hoping that Monday.com can catch on to the frustrations related to the individual plan and make it possible for us to pay a fair amount for an upgrade.

posts.author: pk.afsda (Kurt Osena)
posts.cookedHtml: Oh no, that is too bad Zacheryy. I too wish we didn’t have to pay for 3 seats to use 1 (with upgraded features). I hope that you can somehow work around that. Until then Monday.com fixes this, as toomanybarts mentioned, there are others that provide individual plans. However, that might be tough since I assume your company is currently using Monday.com.

posts.author: lissenburgo (Olly Lissenburg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yea the pricing structure is frustrating, we are currently on 5 seats but could do with 2 more, however, the next package of seats is 10 seats. A doubling in monthly cost just to get 2 more seats is ridiculous.

Hope they do something about it soon!

posts.author: cyowell (Carrie Yowell)
posts.cookedHtml: Couldn’t agree more. That was the first thing I noticed after I started diging into Monday’s features. I am all about automation and task workflow, but I am a one-person show at the moment. That doesn’t mean I don’t use automation and all the other Monday features. There are bound to be many like us who will eventually have teams and would like to have to have the Monday workflows and infrastructure in place to build on as we add employees to our businesses. I think Monday is missing a real opportunity here by not better accommodating this piece of their market.

posts.author: pk.afsda (Kurt Osena)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for sharing lissenburgo. I had someone else express their frustration with the price point beyond the individual plan. It seems that there needs to be a careful look at all the price points to accommodate those of us in the gaps (or those that can’t occupy all the seats but could use the features).

posts.author: pk.afsda (Kurt Osena)
posts.cookedHtml:
That is true cyowell! Again I am a fan of monday.com, I hope that can find more success as the platform is great. Like you said if they can be more considerate of the voices that are not be accommodated with the current pricing plans, then I think they would have a strong appeal.

They should consider charging by the seats used rather than how they have it currently (3, 5, 10, 15, 20 etc.). That would make sense to me.

posts.author: pk.afsda (Kurt Osena)
posts.cookedHtml:
I just reached my max of 200 items under the individual plan. It doesn’t seem sustainable for me to have to invite people and wait for them to join in order for me to keep using monday.com. And I don’t feel it is worth it for me to pay for 3 seats when all I need is one. It looks like I have to start looking at using either asana or click up.

I hope Monday.com does something about this as I have been trying my best to raise awareness to this issue. I would’ve also appreciated some feedback from a representative on this thread, especially since I am not the only one that feels discouraged about the current individual plan price-point.

posts.author: pk.afsda (Kurt Osena)
posts.cookedHtml:
Screen Shot 2021-08-03 at 8.40.30 AM1920×1447 117 KB

Since I"m needing to switch to an affordable individual plan, ClickUp has these prices that they charge per member and works similarly to Monday.com. This type of price plan makes more sense to me.

posts.author: Weiliang (Weiliang)
posts.cookedHtml:
Similar experience. I used monday.com at work and enjoyed the full feature of monday.com. Since I’m on my own now, it doesn’t makes sense for me to pay for 3-seat for my single-seat usage.

And since I’m familiar with monday.com, I would gladly pay for single-seat usage but there’s no such offer available. Free Individual plan isn’t powerful.

When single-seat user begins to manage a team or starts their own company, they would gladly implement monday.com into the process workflow. I do hope monday.com would consider these suggestion.

posts.author: rpark (rpark)
posts.cookedHtml: Just realized today, after using Monday for about a month with all their free trial stuff and enjoying the Gantt view, that the minimum payment to get that is $30 a month since I have to pay for three seats. As a single user that is painful, but more importantly I feel that their marketing is incredibly deceiving. I expected to be able to pay $10 for a single seat after the trial ended. Now I’m highly unlikely to continue using Monday. Bummer.

posts.author: itsworseinjax (Cody)
posts.cookedHtml:
I was just about to switch from ClickUp when I realized that, in order to get formulas, I would have to pay $48/month since there is no 1-seat option! That is INSANE! I would be willing to pay the $16/month due to all of the additional features, but being the only person using the app for my company, there is no way I will stick with it paying that much. Back to ClickUp and searching for an alternative…

I’m really bummed, because I fell in love with Monday. I’m just glad that I realized this now instead of in two weeks after my trial! Agreed with recent user post - deceptive marketing.

posts.author: rarestudio (Victoria Riordan)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for a single person / 2 seat plan. Currently a one-person show here but would love to be able to use all the features without paying $81 AUD / month for 2 seats that I don’t use yet!

posts.author: odd (Laura)
posts.cookedHtml:
Came across this thread whilst pondering over the same issue. We’re a small UK business that is operated by 1 person (me). I needed some software to help with order management and scheduling so signed up to the trial and the product is perfectly meeting my needs.

Now the trial is about to come to an end, I am forced to sign up to a 3 seat plan.

I spoke to monday about it but they said it’s basically just the way it is and they couldn’t help.

I even illustrated how clickup were offering me to sign up for their individual plan at just $5 a month, with many more features than even their $9 plan (which at 3 seat min works out to $27).

I think monday.com is missing a big trick here as I imagine a lot more individuals would be signing up at that kind of pricing.

As for me, I’ll likely be reluctantly moving off of Monday.com due to the pricing structure

posts.author: Aleksei (Aleksei)
posts.cookedHtml:
I loved Monday.com when I worked in a team of developers. I started my solo way, and I can say I will really miss some Monday features as Time tracking, for example. I don’t need a team access, only a couple of guests at a time.

I request a special plan for freelancers like myself:

  • One important Pro feature - Time Tracking
  • 1 user
  • 1-2 guests.
  • Price as for 1 Pro user ($16 per month).

It’s a bit too much to pay for inexistent 4 users. 5-member teams have a great advantage given by Monday pricing over those who work solo, as they pay for existing team members who work and make money.

Of course I understand that for Monday it’s much better to get payment for 5 users even if there’s actually one, the math is quite simple. But in my case (and I guess some others too), it will be just nothing at all, 0 users instead of 1 - thanks to Corona I can’t afford 1 year Pro for 5 users.

And time tracking is the most needed feature for me from Monday (comments on tasks are good too). Maybe it’s better to get a little less money per user but from much more users?
In case if I grow as a company, and Corona-crisis ends (it will once), it will be necessary to move to 5 and more member plans. But for now - not… Your thoughts?

P.S. I have 2 days more to enjoy Monday time tracking of 7 days trial. I’ve tried a few more apps with free time tracking feature, but I’m used to Monday, I like UI here, and I’m willing to pay for that. Just not for something I never use.

posts.author: TheOtherPM (Paul McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey, there is a 2 person plan. We’re on it. I think you have to email support to ask for it to be applied to your account.

posts.author: Aleksei (Aleksei)
posts.cookedHtml: Wow, sounds good. I’ll contact support then to get more info.

posts.author: Aleksei (Aleksei)
posts.cookedHtml:
Well, 2 Users is an interesting plan, but still is a little too powerful. No need for a second user in a freelancer case, guests are enough. Saving money by not paying for an unused feature is very important nowadays, so The 1 User Plan - The Freelancer plan - is a needed thing for me. And, I suppose, for many other people like me, freelancers and self-employed alike.

I’d buy 1 User Pro plan immediately if there was one.

posts.author: alexm (Alex M)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Aleksei - you should want 2x users. All freelancers should have at least a VA/PA or other. Your time is valued at $20, $30, $70, $200 per hour - whatever it is.

Give all those $10 p/h jobs to someone you can trust who will get them done well for you with pride and accuracy. And then you can spend more time doing what you do best, bringing in more $ per hour.

You won’t regret it

Best,
Alex

posts.author: anon34162818 (anon34162818)
posts.cookedHtml: I would favor a little more expensive fully featured Freelancer option, pay for the features but 1 user…as a freelancer the more powerful features are important and paying for the extra users to get them is hard to accept.

posts.author: Toploader (Jon Merchant)
posts.cookedHtml: Yeah, a singer user account would be great and I’d get it along side our business account for personal use, so they are leaving money on the table by not allowing it.

posts.author: Aleksei (Aleksei)
posts.cookedHtml: Alex, thanks for your thoughts I’ve been using the 2-users plan for a month now, but I would prefer start paying for a second user only when he/she appears in my team. Though I see the business scheme of Monday behind it, understand it, and even had actually accepted it by buying the plan, I’d be far more glad to see Monday moving towards self-employed users, not in the opposite direction (by which, by the way, they removed even 2-users plan from the official price table).

posts.author: snicho (Stephen Nicholson)
posts.cookedHtml:
I can definitely see a need for this, particularly from a freelancer developer point of view.

However, I think that having 5 users available for test purposes, eg. for representing Teams, is essential. The difference is that they won’t be real users. I’d set them up with test email addresses only.

posts.author: Aleksei (Aleksei)
posts.cookedHtml: There is a 7-days test period with no limitations of functionality and number of users, it’s already enough. And if you wish testing longer you definitely may buy 5-users plan.

posts.author: Richard94 (Richard Cooke)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m in exactly the same position, having used monday before I love the platform but now as a freelancer I can’t justify the overhead of paying for 3 seats.

If there was an option for a standard or pro plan with 1 seat. or a specific freelancer plan with the subset of the features which are specifically important to freelancers. I would pay for a year subscription now in a heartbeat, as it is I’m currently getting used another platform but I prefer monday.

posts.author: amlstuart (Anthony Stuart)
posts.cookedHtml: Same for this. Used it for myself for a month, was about to buy it, but since I can’t have 1 user I will be finding something else. Seems shortsighted. I might come back to it someday if this is doable.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @amlstuart - Sending you a PM

posts.author: kcorpceo (Arseniy)
posts.cookedHtml: PM me as well ._. Really want to get back to Monday, but can’t justify paying for 2 extra users I won’t have.

posts.author: Yaaatian (Yatian Li)
posts.cookedHtml:
Same here!!!

I love Monday, love those features which are really useful for me to manage my OWN work.

I would pay for the single user pro if there is immediately, but now because my trial ends, and I can’t even use it, but I have to mange all those stuff on my own, so I have to move to asana as it’s free for individual.

posts.author: Cdafter (Chris Dafter)
posts.cookedHtml: I am in the same position, as a sole trader. Have loved the trial period functions of Monday.com Standard/Pro plan, but will have to find something else. Would be willing to pay for 1 user.

posts.author: OLSENFOTO (Christoffer Olsen)
posts.cookedHtml: That would be great! Willing to pay for 1 user pro account.

posts.author: Noelle (Noelle Healy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for the tip about Asana!

I’m the same as every other freelancer here, Monday.com is great but excluding a huge demographic of individual users is phenomenally short-sighted/calculated. Like most, I used the free trial and loved the UI and wanted to purchase the Pro Plan for 1 seat – but seeing they won’t allow means I’ll cut my losses before wasting any more time and energy. It’s not enough to justify paying extortionate rates per 1 user. It’s just not financially viable in 2021 or beyond when people need to cut costs more than ever.

Off to Asana I go! Ty : )

posts.author: mhorlacher (Marc)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m using Monday since about a year at my Institute. Would love to use it for personal projects, but buying 3 seats doesn’t make any sense economically. Shame.

posts.author: toomanybarts (Bart S)
posts.cookedHtml:
In same boat as everyone else on here : used Monday.com at my last place of work - AMAZING and loved it.
Recently set up on my own and my only real option is to upgrade and pay for 3 users?!
There is only me.
Can we have pro-rated plans for just the single freelancer?

This is 2021 and the past 18 months of Covid has moved a LOT of people to freelance and “Portfolio work” (apparently the new name for Consulting - who knew!).

I’ll ping support to find out about this 2 user plan - but even then it hurts as I’ll be paying for a seat I wont use.

posts.author: toomanybarts (Bart S)
posts.cookedHtml: How are you finding Asana?

posts.author: Robpol86 (Robert Pooley)
posts.cookedHtml:
I was about to put down my credit card for the Standard tier to use Gantt charts and integrations (which I use to convert story points to timelines to make gantt charts work). Then I saw there’s a 3 seat minimum when subscribing. That’s too expensive for me when I just use Monday to manage my hobby projects, just for me.

Please consider supporting 1-seat subscriptions for the Standard tier.

posts.author: LSE (Lucas)
posts.cookedHtml:
The free Individual Plan is great for testing, but sooner or later, I’ll hit that 200 items limitation (up to 1000 with referrals), which is definitely not viable for the long run.

I too, would like a paid 1-seat plan.

posts.author: exceed37 (christian)
posts.cookedHtml: This is the reason why I have not upgraded to any yearly plan. I really like monday, but its difficult to justify the price for multiple users that I dont need. I only need one users and I dont even need guests, but i need time tracking and other features. Sometimes I wish I saw the license model before investing so much time into this service or I probably would not use my time on it. I almost think it is kind of rude to have an individual plan just to be kind of forced over to a plan where you have to pay for multiple users to use the software since you will be hitting the limits sooner or later.

posts.author: pk.afsda (Kurt Osena)
posts.cookedHtml:
I posted about the need for an individual plan myself, and ran into this post. I’m glad to see more users feeling for a upgraded individual plan with a price point that makes sense. As much as I love the interface of Monday.com I cannot justify paying for unused seats when I do not plan to have a team. I would prefer to use Monday.com as an individual. After exhausting my 200 items for the free individual plan, I had no choice but to switch to ClickUp. I really hope Monday.com hears the needs of individuals like ourselves because I think they are missing out on a wider market.

Hopefully something can be done about this soon.

posts.author: jaimelicea (Jaime Licea)
posts.cookedHtml: Same for this. I really want to buy an individual plan, but it make no sense to pay for extra persons that are not there. Hope you can contact me and give me a solution.

posts.author: Clifton (Clifton Crawley)
posts.cookedHtml:
It’s amazing that I am looking at this thread that is months old and I’m running into the same issue. On one hand, I understand that Monday.com was really built for teams. That’s why they have minimums. On the other hand, I would like the software to grow with me.

I like the trial of Monday. However, I don’t wish to pay for seats I won’t be using just yet. Not that I can’t extra monthly subscription; I just won’t be in business much longer if I just waste money.

I’m looking at Notion and it looks pretty promising - especially for smaller teams and freelancers.

posts.author: JustTiffany (Tiffany)
posts.cookedHtml:
Me too! Still nothing!
I even emailed about a two person plan and they suggested the 3 person plan which is too expensive.
I don’t even want to use it for a business, I literally just want to use it for household management. I’m happy to pay for it because having a good tool is important to me and there are a lot of features I enjoy, but I’m not sure I am willing to pay THAT much for it.

posts.author: uriyasama (Uriya Enmar)
posts.cookedHtml: Hopefully the more people respond to this post the better the chances someone would listen. No other tool works as well as monday, and it sucks that I have to pay for more users than I actually need. Paying for more users makes the price so crazy that I’d have to move to one of the abysmal competitors

posts.author: katiefrigginburk (Katie Burk)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m also here for this! I’ve used Monday.com with several companies in the past, and I’m a huge fan. But I’m on my own as a freelancer now, and I don’t want to pay for seats that will never be used! I’d absolutely pay for Pro if they offered just 1 seat. I might go with Trello until (fingers crossed) they offer this option.

posts.author: tjcahill (Tiernan)
posts.cookedHtml:
In a similar situation here: I’ve been trying out the Student plan to manage the many projects I’m involved with as a doctoral student, and finding it really useful, to the point where I would happily pay for a premium individual plan once I graduate. However, looking ahead and seeing that I would have to buy a minimum of three licenses right off the bat has me looking for alternatives.

Even though I would likely eventually be looking to use Monday in a small team (and thus, be in the market for 5-10 seats), I simply can’t justify this without the option to build out my own workflow as an individual first, nor can I justify the recurring expense of three times more licenses than I am using. While I understand that the platform is geared towards collaboration, this seems short-sighted insofar as potential future customers don’t have a real opportunity to get comfortable with the platform on their own terms.

posts.author: Sultan (Sultan Khogeer)
posts.cookedHtml: Same here, I would love a pro plan with one seat. This the only thing holding me from fully getting onboard.

posts.author: YannickVdH (Yannick Van den Houdt)
posts.cookedHtml: Same goes for me! I would love to have the 1 seat pro plan. I contacted support and they didn’t offer the 2 person pro plan to me. I need the formula option to calculate my invoices and the integration with Stripe is lovely. Really hope to see this in the near future.

posts.author: itsworseinjax (Cody)
posts.cookedHtml:
I was just about to switch from ClickUp when I realized that, in order to get formulas, I would have to pay $48/month since there is no 1-seat option! That is INSANE! I would be willing to pay the $16/month due to all of the additional features, but being the only person using the app for my company, there is no way I will stick with it paying that much. Back to ClickUp and searching for an alternative…

I’m really bummed, because I fell in love with Monday. But, again… not paying that.

posts.author: rarestudio (Victoria Riordan)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for one-seat plans! Even though i’m currently a team of one, I really want to make use of everything that the pro plan offers, however being in Australia that amounts to $81 per month for me for 2 seats that I don’t use (yet).

posts.author: barlesh (Bar Leshem)
posts.cookedHtml: Would love to get a pro account - but only is a 1 seat is available. MAKE IT HAPPEN

posts.author: Daveooo (Dave Oudshoorn)
posts.cookedHtml:
How is this still not an option… So many freelancers are willing to start using a product, and probably grow into more expensive plans. You’re just alienating a potential new customers that are ready to bring their money to you. Also our clients might be potential fans of your product and take a subscription for their own use.

I’m a freelancer and I’d love to be able to offer this for 15-20 bucks/month, but now it’s closer to 50/month for three seats of which I only use one. That’s insane.

Please make this happen.

posts.author: dtsno2020 (Desi's Tropical Sno Shack)
posts.cookedHtml:
Same boat here. Right now I use Asana’s free plan, but I’d switch over if monday offered individual plans.

I get watching the bottom line. Why not charge like $24 for a pro individual plans vs the usual $16 per user? You’d be able to nab a TON of business that currently goes to other project management software. Instant sales. Individuals can’t justify $48 a month. Give them something they can afford, and watch your numbers jump. Plus you can capitalize when they grow.

I’d recommend Monday to a lot of friends, but I don’t because of the cost. If you guys ever offer a solo plan, I’d be happy to spread the word.

posts.author: Wh1skeyJack (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml: wow. Apparently Monday just hates freelancers, or hates money? I’m wanting to send them my money for a 1 seat plan, but they can’t be bothered to make that happen? Who is running this show? Why do they hate money so much? I have tried a number of other apps like this, but like Monday the best. they would literally be in category of ONE and corner the market for freelancers if they allowed for 1 seat plan. Hello? Anybody from Monday actually read these things?

posts.author: cjwind (CWTsai)
posts.cookedHtml: Same here. I’ve tried for free and monday is good for me, so I want to buy a plan for my own work. I like calendar, timeline view, integration and automate, but don’t support in free plan. I’d like to buy standard plan with one seat, but don’t like to pay another two seats I don’t need. I’d go to try Asana…

posts.author: Daveooo (Dave Oudshoorn)
posts.cookedHtml:
Still the only option to pay €36/month for just a (small) client, and adding cost to that if I want to offer other shared boards to other clients of mine.

Why is there still not a viable freelance (the ones that manage many small clients) option?

posts.author: smlewman (Sadie Lewman)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 to more fully-featured single user plans. I started using Monday to track my own work at my company— currently they don’t provide any tool for personal time/work management (only release management for dev) so several employees have been left to our own devices. Monday has been the perfect solution for me, but I just simply will not pay $30-$60 per month for two empty seats.

I need a Gantt chart view, so would prefer the Standard plan, but I’m frankly shocked that the dependency column is locked behind Pro. I also need the ability to create a dashboard conglomerating at least 5 boards, since I am resourced to multiple projects at once, so the individual plan currently offered is a nonstarter for me on several aspects.

As others have said, I’m going to try Asana for now until monday.com offers something more valuable and useful for individuals.

posts.author: anniec (Annie)
posts.cookedHtml: Please consider allowing teams or one or two users to purchase paid level plans. I would love to make use of the features but can’t justify purchasing three licenses when two would never get used.

posts.author: Yaaatian (Yatian Li)
posts.cookedHtml:
Please have individual pro plan

I love those features in the standard/pro plan, but don’t have a team yet. So the 3 seats minimum seems too expensive for myself.

So I’m wondering whether Monday will establish individual pro plan for single users.

posts.author: AlexSavchuk (Alex Savchuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Yaaatian

My warmest welcome to the community! Would you be able to clarify if you are looking for an individual pro plan in terms of our pricing? I’m afraid the section you posted this topic in is meant for API and App Framework-related questions to help developers build amazing apps

For now, I am moving this thread to the appropriate section for general monday.com feature requests, and we appreciate your feedback on this.

-Alex

posts.author: YannickVdH (Yannick Van den Houdt)
posts.cookedHtml:
As a freelancer I would love to use the Pro plan to improve my workflow. I’m using the trial version now and it is super helpful with the formula option and all the integrations. At the moment there is only an option to have the Pro plan with 3 seats. The extra overhead cost of 2 unused seats is a bit to much for me.
I think there are a lot of other freelancers who would like to use a pro plan but don’t want to pay for unused seats.

posts.author: vYuWRmFXe6REcFKM46gU (katy)
posts.cookedHtml: I completely agree. I would have loved to pay a bit extra to use time tracking and other tools that are perfect for freelancing but I can’t afford to pay for unused seats. This is a shame and I’ll be looking to other applications that offer something that can be geared towards freelancers.

posts.author: nemojack (Neil Jacklin)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this? I’d purchase a single seat Pro subscription!

posts.author: mmckenna72 (matthew mckenna)
posts.cookedHtml:
I will never sign up for a 3 seat plan just so I can unlock some features, but I would be happy to pay for a single seat plan. I think there are probably thousands of freelancers like me out there who have the same view.

Come on guys, why are you still refusing to do this??

posts.author: sheaobertos (Shea)
posts.cookedHtml: Just wanted to add my voice to this… I hope a single-seat/freelancer/self-employed plan will be added. I doubt they are working on it though. Must not be supported by their financial projections. Hard to believe, given the number of people without a team who would be willing to pay for a single-seat plan and are instead giving the company no money. Hoping that if enough of us express interest, it could tip the scales, but I’m guessing that isn’t the issue. They want to focus on larger teams; I imagine the customer-support to fee ratio is much better or something like that. Sad. Kicking myself for not looking at the plans more closely before getting my workspace all set up.

posts.author: Peace (David Michael Bernstein)
posts.cookedHtml: I think your company is missing a HUGE opportunity. The plans you currently offer demonstrate, to me at least, that someone at the highest levels of Monday.com needs to re-assess ‘the market’ and how your platform can be (and should be) tailored to meet the real world needs of the sole-proprietor and single-member LLCs. We don’t need more than one seat! I’m happily a company of ‘one’. Yet, companies my size can benefit from ‘collaboration’ even if we’re only talking to ourselves. One-man and one-woman shops represent an enormous untapped (potential) new highly lucrative customer base for Monday.com. Based on the latest data from the U.S. Bureau Of Labor Statistics, there are literally millions of individuals just like me in dire need of the amazing capabilities that you have developed. The fact that you offer a ‘free’ INDIVIDUAL plan but not a paid INDIVIDUAL plan is really a bizarre oversight in my opinion. The limitations of the free plan are exactly why I’m willing to migrate to the paid plan. But why should I be compelled to pay for TWO additional seats that I have no use for? It’s preposterous… Your free plan understands the reality of the solitary business owner. Your paid plan obviously should too.

posts.author: headsupst (Jared)
posts.cookedHtml: So we still can’t sign up with just a single user? I need views only available in the Pro plan, but it’s just me, and I’m not paying for three users. I’ve been on Free and would love to upgrade, but need the option to pay for only myself. Currently looking for alternatives if anyone has suggestions, I need a Map View that I can integrate with boards

posts.author: Daveooo (Dave Oudshoorn)
posts.cookedHtml:
Two years later and the pricing plan is still being ignored. Support chat says “Upvote the feature on the community forum”, but pricing plans should not be on the features list. It should be business strategy, and they keep failing to see that the entry step into Monday makes no sense for freelancers.

Sure you’re getting a few that are now overpaying, but you’re missing 70-90% who just find a competitors product when they find out they have to pay for three seats.

posts.author: fairydud (Fairy Dud)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for sharing this! I’ll likely end up moving to something like this. Looks like we’re still two-years out from your comment and still no offer for an individual plan.

posts.author: mcw (Madison F.)
posts.cookedHtml: I would LOVE to be able to use Monday.com for my business, but I’m a solopreneur, and I can’t justify paying triple the price for 2 seats I don’t need. PLEASE rethink your pricing model! There’s no reason not to simply charge per seat like so many other business software platforms do. You’re missing out on my business and surely many others’.

posts.author: ModCon (Mod Con)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m an entrepreneur, starting a business alone. It will be a while until I am able to hire any help. It is precisely for this reason that I am searching for a project management tool that allows me to set up dependencies and automations. As one person wearing all the hats, I want to be able automate as much as humanly possible.

With this and some of my other requirements, Monday fits the bill for the most part. I was a bit frustrated to find that the pro plan was required in order to run automations and dependencies, given that I’m still at the setup stage and not wanting to have too many expenses, but I decided that the investment was worth it. Until I discovered that there aren’t ANY paid plans available for anything less than 3 seats.

Wth Monday? It’s a pretty awful way to treat small businesses, entrepreneurs and freelancers. I’ve seen excuses that Monday is designed for collaboration, but come on, all project management has collaboration capabilities. It is still a powerful software to use with no collaboration at all.

Please reconsider your attitude toward small businesses, and try to remember that small businesses grow into big businesses. When I spend time an effort setting up workflows in a different project management tool, when the time comes to upgrade the amount of seats it’s pretty unlikely I’ll migrate to this service. Why would you want to bias people against your software like this…?

posts.author: ubaldot (Ubaldo)
posts.cookedHtml:
I running a hobby alone, and I am fine with the free plan. I don’t need too much. The only thing that I miss is the Gantt chart.

Given that this is the only thing I need of the paid plan, I would gladly pay 10€/Month to upgrade and get it - I don’t really need any other additional features - but I won’t pay 30€/Month only because I need a Gantt in addition to what the free plan currently offer.

If they add the possibility of adding ONE seat only I think they would get my subscription. Otherwise, no, sorry. Unless they won’t add Gantt charts in the free plan
My workaround now is to use Date column to remark deadlines and give up a visual representation of timelines.

posts.author: bobinsana (Brian)
posts.cookedHtml: Man I’m in exactly the same situation, just months behind. I’m so exhausted finally finding this answer that I can just say I agree. I think this is the perfect app for me, which is a big deal, but the pricing structure kills it. did you find a better solution? merakimeadow@pm.me

posts.author: bobinsana (Brian)
posts.cookedHtml: if a couple people want to jump on a subscription I might be down, hit me up

posts.author: Deuce (Tracy Cisneros)
posts.cookedHtml: Really hoping a single seat option becomes available for the Basic and Standard plans. I’ve loved using Monday’s WM system during the trial period and while I prefer the Standard for its automation and integration features, I can’t justify paying for 3 seats especially considering I can get those same features along with Timeline, Gantt, Kanban, and Time Tracking on Notion’s Plus plan for $8. If Monday offered a single seat Standard plan and kept the same pricing structure ($9 for Basic and $12 for Standard), I would switch over from Notion in a heartbeat.

posts.author: christopherrobin1024 (Alex)
posts.cookedHtml: Do you think people who choose the Pro plan don’t care about money? I switched from Pipedrive, where I paid $25 for the same functionality, and in Monday it’s $100 — four times the difference! It feels like a rip-off…

posts.author: SeaLight (Eric Hartmann)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am requesting that there is a single-seat option for freelancers/solopreneurs. I believe that if you put out a survey among those businesses that you would see a massive uptick in users.

You could structure it this way:

Only let 1 seat able to buy the Basic or Standard plans.
Basic:
1 seat: $15/mo
3 seats: $27/mo

Standard:
1 seat: $19/mo
3 seats: $36/mo

By doing this, you would have people who need 2 seats automatically jump to a 3-seat plan because it would be adding value by making it $9/seat or $12/seat thereby, increasing your profit margins and providing more value to those businesses. Also by structuring the seats this way, you’re showing that your business is supporting freelancers and solopreneurs that want to functionality of Monday and are able to afford it.

posts.author: SeaLight (Eric Hartmann)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am requesting that there is a single-seat option for freelancers/solopreneurs. I believe that if you put out a survey among those businesses that you would see a massive uptick in users.

You could structure it this way:

3 seats: $27/mo

Standard:

3 seats: $36/mo

By doing this, you would have people who need 2 seats automatically jump to a 3-seat plan because it would be adding value by making it $9/seat or $12/seat thereby, increasing your profit margins and providing more value to those businesses. Also by structuring the seats this way, you’re showing that your business is supporting freelancers and solopreneurs that want to functionality of Monday and are able to afford it.

posts.author: ctlockey (Cameron Lockey)
posts.cookedHtml:
Currently the minimum number of seats I can purchase is 3 seats, making the barrier of entry pretty high for small startup companies that are bootstrapping things without funding. I’d love to use the Dev product, but there’s no way I can swing $27/month x 12 = $324 up front when I haven’t made my first dollar with my business yet. This is especially true since it will be a long time until I need 3x seats.

I’d love to be able to buy seats a la carte - 1 seat at a time. Or at least have a single-seat package, for the Dev product.

In the meantime I’ll be making Free Work Management work for me.

posts.author: Msimo14 (Mitchell Simonsen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Totally agree Cameron. We’re a company that needs 6 seats. So therefor we have to pay $912 extra dollars a year for ONE extra seat? That’s silly. If we’d known this before hand it would have swayed us away from Monday.com.

Edit: Ultimately while we would pay for that extra seat, in the end it makes more sense to just have him as a guest and his tasks checked off by his supervisor. If the idea behind not selling individual seats was to artificially inflate the price- you’ve managed to instead drive down how much we spend.

posts.author: Jordy (Robers)
posts.cookedHtml: Total agree, it is even statistically worse going from 5 to 10, its a 100% forced increase and doubling the spending per year. Considering when having 5 members you are considered a small company. To load small companies up with a doubling yearly fee is just crazy. They seem not to have much concern about this as they probably assume no one will leave and set up a entire system at a competitor saas.

posts.author: Jordy (Robers)
posts.cookedHtml:
how about not forcing organisations to work with compromises as the fee is already high enough to be able to add single seats.

It is a bit ridicolous to ask from a 30 seat company to work with mediocre solutions only so Monday.com can keep a unfair price structure. Some increments are 100%, which does not even make sense.

posts.author: 911dutygear (Braeden)
posts.cookedHtml:
We are not a large business but as we grow, we would like to add just enough users as we need for our team. This whole “required seats” thing is actually super frustrating and make us want to find a different SAAS project management service.

No business in the planet wants to pay for something they don’t need. Like every other software we use charge a certain amount for certain quantities like everyone else!

1-3 $
4-5 $
6-10 $
11-20 $
20 - 30 $
Etc

It’s really not hard. You are loosing customers because of this…
